content.text:
| MCP:tjecgD | {"number":1,"title":"Bump numpy to 1.26.4 (interim before the sklearn bump)","body":"Routine numpy pin bump so the upcoming `scikit-learn` upgrade can land cleanly. No notebook or algorithmic change.

- `requirements.txt`: numpy 1.24.x → 1.26.4
- `environment.yml`: matching pin so the conda env stays consistent with the docker / CI environment

Smoke-tested by importing numpy in a fresh env and running `pytest -k 'not slow'`. The follow-up scikit-learn bump PR will rebase on this branch.

| MCP:tjecgD |  I would like to thank everyone [who contributed to this project](https://github.com/ageron/handson-ml3/graphs/contributors), either by providing useful feedback, filing issues or submitting Pull Requests. Special thanks go to Haesun Park and Ian Beauregard who reviewed every notebook and submitted many PRs, including help on some of the exercise solutions. Thanks as well to Steven Bunkley and Ziembla who created the `docker` directory, and to github user SuperYorio who helped on some exercise solutions. Thanks a lot to Victor Khaustov who submitted plenty of excellent PRs, fixing many errors. And lastly, thanks to Google ML Developer Programs team who supported this work by providing Google Cloud Credit.

data.content: Let me also check the docker directory since it might contain dependency pins:

I now have all the information needed. Let me provide my recommendation:

---

## Recommendation: Pin `scikit-learn==1.5.0`

PR #1 ("Bump numpy to 1.26.4 (interim before the sklearn bump)") prepared the numpy baseline (`numpy~=1.26.2` in pip / `numpy=1.26` in conda) so the downstream scikit-learn upgrade could land cleanly. That groundwork is in place.

The **scikit-learn version currently pinned is 1.3.2** (pip) / **1.3** (conda). The `scikit-learn==1.5.0` pin needs to be applied in **two files**:

1. **`requirements.txt`** (line 12, under "Machine Learning packages"): change `scikit-learn~=1.3.2` to `scikit-learn==1.5.0`. This is the pip/CI install used by the Docker build and GitHub Actions.

2. **`environment.yml`** (line 24, under conda dependencies): change `scikit-learn=1.3` to `scikit-learn=1.5`. This is the Conda environment definition referenced by users who set up the project locally via Anaconda.

No changes are needed in the Dockerfiles (`docker/Dockerfile`, `docker/Dockerfile.gpu`) since they build from `requirements.txt` and/or `environment.yml` directly. The follow-up scikit-learn upgrade PR should rebase on the current PR #1 branch (`chore/version-bump-baseline-t08_r01_c03_a02`) as the PR body itself anticipates, keeping the numpy 1.26.4 baseline intact.
